extra.message: Stored and Reflected XSS: User-supplied 'comment' from POST body (line 34) is stored in the database and retrieved unsanitized, then passed to render_template at line 40. User-supplied 'q' query parameter (line 36) is also passed to the template unsanitized at line 42. Both are rendered without HTML escaping, allowing injection of arbitrary JavaScript.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Stored and Reflected XSS: User-supplied 'comment' from POST body (line 10) is stored and retrieved unsanitized, then passed to render_template at line 16. User-supplied 'q' query parameter (line 12) is also passed to the template unsanitized at line 18. Both are rendered without HTML escaping.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Path Traversal to Arbitrary File Read: The regex filter at line 10-11 uses re.match with pattern r'/\\.\\.\\/\\.\\.\\/' which only matches '../..' at the beginning of the path. Traversal sequences in the middle of the path (e.g., /foo/../../etc/passwd) bypass the check. The user-controlled self.path is concatenated directly to the OS working directory at line 14, and the resulting path is opened and served at line 22 without any canonicalization or sandboxing, allowing reading of any file on the system.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF): The URL validation at line 14 uses string.startswith to check for 'https://insecurewebapp.com', but this can be bypassed. An attacker can supply a URL like 'https://insecurewebapp.com@evil.com' or 'https://insecurewebapp.com.evil.com' which will pass the startswith check while urllib.request.urlopen resolves it to the attacker's host. The fetched content is returned directly to the client.

Length Extension Attack Vulnerability: The sign_for_payment function constructs a MAC by computing SHA-256(secret + payment_information) at lines 17-18. This prefix-MAC construction using SHA-256 (a Merkle-Damgård hash) is vulnerable to length extension attacks. An attacker who knows the original message and its hash can compute a valid hash for the original message with arbitrary data appended, without knowing the secret. Use HMAC instead.

Length Extension Attack Vulnerability: The sign_for_payment function constructs a MAC by computing SHA-256(secret + payment_information) at lines 12-13. This prefix-MAC construction using SHA-256 (a Merkle-Damgård hash) is vulnerable to length extension attacks, allowing an attacker to forge valid signatures for tampered payment data.

Disabled SSL/TLS Certificate Hostname Verification: context.check_hostname is set to False at line 6, disabling hostname verification in SSL connections. This makes the TLS connection vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks because the server's certificate hostname is never validated against the expected hostname.

Hardcoded Flask Secret Key: The Flask SECRET_KEY is hardcoded as 'qwerty12345' at line 52. This key is used for session signing, CSRF protection, and other cryptographic operations. A hardcoded key allows attackers to forge session cookies and bypass authentication if the key is known.

Hardcoded Flask Secret Key: The Flask SECRET_KEY is hardcoded as '55555' at line 26. This key is used for session signing and cryptographic operations. A predictable, hardcoded key allows attackers to forge session cookies.

SQL Injection: User input post_id is concatenated directly into a SQL query string at line 70: 'SELECT * FROM posts WHERE id = '+post_id. The regex validation at line 67 using re.match with r'^\d+$' is insufficient — it only checks that the string starts with digits but fails if the entire input is not numeric (re.match anchors ^ to start of string only; when multi-line flag re.M is set it also matches after newlines, potentially allowing newline-injected SQL). No parameterized query is used. Note: This code resides in a function override with an indefinite recursion bug (line 61) that would prevent execution, but the injection pattern is present and would be exploitable if the recursion is fixed.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: Reflected XSS: The 'username' variable decoded from the user's session cookie (line 26) is interpolated directly into HTML response at line 37 via 'Hello %s' % username without any HTML escaping. An attacker who can control the contents of the encrypted cookie — or who can exploit the use of unauthenticated AES-CBC encryption (no HMAC/MAC) via ciphertext bit-flipping — could inject arbitrary JavaScript into the page.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Unverified Message Integrity / Unauthenticated Encryption: The decrypt method at lines 16-20 uses AES-CBC without any Message Authentication Code (MAC) via HMAC. This makes the encrypted cookie vulnerable to tampering — attackers can perform ciphertext bit-flipping attacks to manipulate the decrypted plaintext without knowing the key. This could be leveraged to inject XSS payloads, escalate privileges, or bypass authentication checks.

cwe: CWE-347: Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature
